title: Reclassify res.send family — FileWrite → HtmlOutput (ATOMIC with test edits)
scope_summary: In TYPESCRIPT_AST_SINKS (taint.rs), reclassified the `*.send(...)` family — `('reply','send')` (Fastify), `('res','send')` / `('Response','send')` / `('response','send')` (NestJS Express-style + Response-builder forms) — from sink_type FileWrite to a NEW HtmlOutput AstSinkPattern. Kept `('reply','redirect')`, `('reply','header')`, `('res','redirect')`, `('res','json')`, `('Response','json')`, `('response','redirect')`, `('response','json')` as FileWrite (semantically navigation/header-emit/JSON-emit, not Xss). Updated the M2 deferred-reclassification carry-forward note inline to reflect M3 retirement of the `*.send` partial flip. Atomic with 3 test assertion updates at rr_framework_integ_test.rs:168, :248, :301.

loc_delta_actual.net_source: +22 / -14 source code (one merged FileWrite entry split into two banks: reduced FileWrite + new HtmlOutput); test churn neutral on count but variant-flipped

kept_unchanged_in_W1_M1_nextjs_block: ('Response','redirect')     -> FileWrite (untouched per spec — App Router alias)
bank_flip_diff.comment_retirement_summary: The W1-M3 NestJS block comment (taint.rs:2003-2027 pre-M3) was updated to retire the `Express-style res.send|redirect|json(...)` listing — it now reads `Express-style res.redirect|json(...)`. A new note appended to the same block explains the M3 reclassification rationale. The M2 HtmlOutput block comment (taint.rs:2052-2064 pre-M3) was updated to retire the `a future reclassification milestone may flip these entries to HtmlOutput; that is out of scope for M2` line — replaced with a note documenting M3's `*.send` flip and clarifying that innerHTML/document.write/dangerouslySetInnerHTML remain FileWrite-projected pending a future reclassification.

test_assertion_updates.assert_message_before: expected at least one FileWrite sink for reply.send
test_assertion_updates.assert_message_after: expected at least one HtmlOutput sink for reply.send
test_assertion_updates.rationale: reply.send(tainted) emits HTML body — Xss vector, not navigation. Now wired to HtmlOutput per M3 bank flip.

test_assertion_updates.assert_message_before: expected at least one FileWrite sink for res.send
test_assertion_updates.assert_message_after: expected at least one HtmlOutput sink for res.send

test_assertion_updates.assert_message_before: expected at least one FileWrite sink for Response.send (builder)
test_assertion_updates.assert_message_after: expected at least one HtmlOutput sink for Response.send (builder)
test_assertion_updates.rationale: Premortem identified 2 of 3 send-test assertions; M1 pre-flight grep surfaced the 3rd (this one) by scanning `TaintSinkType::FileWrite` directly. Without this update the test would have transitioned RED post-bank-flip, breaking the regression guard.

pre_flight_grep_post_change_verification.command_1_interpretation: Hits are inside string-literal FP fixtures (path_traversal_string_literal_fp.{js,ts}) — same as M1 pre-flight grep snapshot. The FP-clean property holds whether res.send is FileWrite or HtmlOutput; the fixture's purpose is to ensure neither yields a finding when only inside a string. M3's reclassification does not affect these FP fixtures.

pre_flight_grep_post_change_verification.command_3_interpretation: The 8 remaining FileWrite assertions exercise non-`.send` shapes (NextResponse.redirect 59; NextResponse.json 84; bare redirect helper 112; dangerouslySetInnerHTML 138; reply.redirect 193; reply.header 218; res.redirect 273; Response.redirect 330) which M3 KEEPS as FileWrite per spec. All 8 still pass post-M3 — confirmed via `cargo test -p tldr-core --release --test rr_framework_integ_test` 18/18 GREEN.
pre_flight_grep_post_change_verification.verification_status_summary: ZERO additional FileWrite-near-send hits beyond the 3 known cases (lines 168/248/301). All 3 known cases were updated atomically with the bank flip. No 4th case discovered. ssrf gate per `additive_bank_change_only` validator mandate satisfied.

validator_mandate_compliance.m3_test_reclassification_atomic: PASS — bank flip + 3 test assertion updates staged in a single commit; no intermediate commit between them.
validator_mandate_compliance.additive_bank_change_only: PASS — M3 is the contract-permitted single non-additive milestone (sink_type field reclassification on existing entries). Pre-flight grep run pre-change (M1) and post-change (this report); zero unanticipated hits.
validator_mandate_compliance.string_literal_regression_zero_new_fps: PASS — javascript/typescript path_traversal_string_literal_fp pass per cargo test capture; res.send inside string literals does not fire (substring path lookups guard against in-string matches via tree-sitter literal node detection).
validator_mandate_compliance.e2e_test_preservation_mandatory: PASS — no test_e2e_* tests touched; cargo test for rr_framework_integ_test confirms 18/18 GREEN including 8 untouched FileWrite-asserting tests.
validator_mandate_compliance.m2_m3_m4_serial: PASS — M3 executed serially after M2 (HEAD: f838387). M4 has not started; serialization preserved.

premortem_E1_resolution: Premortem identified 2 of 3 send-test assertions requiring atomic update (lines 168, 248). M1 pre-flight grep surfaced the 3rd at line 301 (nestjs_response_builder_send_via_compute_taint). M3 updated all 3 atomically with the bank flip. Without the line 301 update, the bank flip would have transitioned that test from GREEN to RED (it was asserting FileWrite but the M3 flip moves response.send to HtmlOutput), breaking the regression guard.

files_NOT_staged_intentionally: Cargo.lock — never staged per standing rule
All other working-tree dirty files (unrelated continuum/* JSON drift) — not part of M3
